title: The First Night
setting: Night has fallen on the first day after the earthquake. The sky is bright orange from the fires consuming the city to the east. Golden Gate Park has become a massive refugee camp. Rosa and Genthe sit near a small campfire. The children are asleep. For the first time all day, there is stillness.

script.speaker: Arnold Genthe
script.text: The children are sleeping well, at least.

script.speaker: Rosa Castellano
script.text: They were so tired. Elena cried for an hour and then just, she was gone.

script.speaker: Arnold Genthe
script.text: You should try to sleep too. You have been on your feet since before the sun came up.

script.speaker: Rosa Castellano
script.text: I keep watching it. I know I should look away but I can't stop watching it.

script.speaker: Arnold Genthe
script.text: I know. I have been doing the same thing.

script.speaker: Rosa Castellano
script.text: Our street is in there somewhere. All of it, the furniture, the photographs, my mother's dishes. It's all in there.

script.speaker: Rosa Castellano
script.text: I'm sorry. You lost your studio. Everything in it, I imagine.

script.speaker: Arnold Genthe
script.text: My negatives, my equipment. Years of work. But you asked me something this morning and I have been thinking about it all day.

script.speaker: Arnold Genthe
script.text: You asked why I was taking pictures instead of helping. I didn't have a good answer then. I'm not sure I have one now.

script.speaker: Rosa Castellano
script.text: You found us before dark. That was the answer.
